articles.title: AUTODESK IS SUING GOOGLE OVER THE NAME OF ITS FLOW AI VIDEOMAKER
articles.summary: Autodesk is suing Google over trademark infringement, claiming Google's AI video generator, Flow, will confuse customers with Autodesk's own "Flow" brand of AI-enabled filmmaking tools launched in 2022. Autodesk alleges that after requesting that Google stop using the name, Google misrepresented its intentions and filed trademark applications, even though confusion between the products had already occurred on social media. The company is seeking a court order to block Google from using the Flow trademark and unspecified damages.

articles.title: WEBFLOW MEETS CLAUDE: DESIGN, STRUCTURE, AND MANAGE SITES RIGHT FROM CLAUDE
articles.summary: Webflow has launched an official connector for Claude that lets users design pages, manage CMS content, and structure websites directly via AI prompts. The connector enables Claude to perform real actions in Webflow, including bulk CMS updates, SEO audits, managing design systems, and applying custom code changes. Users need a paid Claude plan and can configure the connector's permissions to balance automation with oversight for their workflow.

articles.title: FACEBOOK PROFILE PICTURES CAN NOW BE ANIMATED WITH AI
articles.summary: Meta has introduced AI-powered features on Facebook that animate profile pictures, restyle photos in Stories and Memories with text prompts or preset styles, and add animated backgrounds to text posts. The updates aim to boost creativity and help revive “OG Facebook” appeal, as Meta seeks to make the platform more culturally relevant again.

articles.title: THE DESIGN THAT STAYS IN WORDS
articles.summary: Many design leaders talk about quality and strategy but have lost touch with actual design craft, making them unable to recognize or build good work. While these leaders focus on metrics and facilitating meetings, the real value comes from designers who maintain hands-on skills and can make tangible decisions that improve products. The gap is widening between those who only talk about design and those who actually practice it, with the latter being more valuable in today's fast-moving environment.

articles.title: WHY USERS GET LOST IN YOUR PRODUCT
articles.summary: Product taxonomy directly impacts user adoption, conversion, and growth across PLG companies. Without deliberate structure (ideally 2-3 hierarchical levels, like Product > Tool > Feature), confusion compounds from pricing pages to analytics, leading to drops in conversion, slower time-to-value, and misalignment across teams. Simple, consistent classification systems enable users to learn faster, teams to build cohesively, and growth to scale without accumulating "clarity debt."

articles.title: THE 80% JOB: HOW DESIGN LEADS ARE USING AI — AND IT'S NOT ABOUT MOCKUPS
articles.summary: Design leadership is far less about crafting pixels and far more about communication, mediation, justification, and stakeholder alignment — often leaving little to no time for actual design work. AI's biggest opportunity for design leads isn't generating mockups, but reclaiming time through automating meetings, research synthesis, documentation, and quick “vibe-coded” prototypes that help win alignment, de-risk ideas, and free up space for the strategic judgment only humans can provide.

articles.title: AI-POWERED ONLINE STORE GENERATION
articles.summary: Genstore lets users launch an AI-powered online store in just 2 minutes with a single prompt. The platform offers ready-to-sell products across categories such as fashion, beauty, and home decor.

articles.title: UNIFIED WORKSPACE TO BUILD, DEPLOY, AND SCALE APPLICATIONS
articles.summary: CreateOS is a unified execution environment where ideas move seamlessly from concept to live deployment and monetization.

articles.title: CREATE ART AND VISUALS FROM TEXT
articles.summary: Unleash your creativity with IconScout AI's powerful text-to-image generator. Type your idea and watch it come to life.

articles.title: LINEAR DESIGN VS. MINIMALISM, BRUTALISM, AND NEUMORPHISM
articles.summary: Linear design emerged from decades of UX research and represents a practical approach to minimalism, featuring monochrome color palettes, single sans-serif fonts, and subtle animations that prioritize usability and accessibility. Unlike brutalism, which uses extreme contrast for bold visual impact, or neumorphism, which creates artificial lighting effects through shadows, linear design focuses on clear boundaries and visual affordances that enhance user experience. Linear design's minimalist traits serve functional rather than purely artistic purposes, making it more effective for usability than other design trends.

articles.title: CLAUDE CODE GUIDE FOR DESIGNERS
articles.summary: Claude Code is a coding agent that executes plain English instructions, enabling designers without coding backgrounds to build and deploy functional apps in days rather than months. This guide covers installation, basic terminal commands, project creation, iterative prompting, version control with GitHub, and live deployment through Vercel. Advanced steps include adding authentication, databases, and AI features using services like Supabase to transform static sites into full web applications.

articles.title: THIS IS THE FIRST SMARTPHONE DESIGNED BY KIDS
articles.summary: AT&T's amiGO Jr. is a kid-focused smartphone built with Samsung that gives parents advanced controls over screen time, apps, location sharing, and school-hour restrictions via their own devices. While it offers strong parental safeguards and solid specs, the phone looks like a standard Android device, raising debate over whether a controlled smartphone is better than no phone at all — and whether its design truly feels kid-friendly.

articles.title: WHY AI VIDEO EDITING IS THE SKILL I'M FINALLY LEARNING IN 2026
articles.summary: Wondershare's Filmora offers AI-generated video features, allowing users to create videos from simple prompts.

articles.title: A BRIEF HISTORY OF APP ICONS FROM APPLE'S CREATOR STUDIO
articles.summary: A recent update to a macOS icon collection to include Apple's new Creator Studio family revealed how these app icons have evolved over time.

articles.title: TOM GAULD'S BOOK PHYSICS FOR CATS SPINS SCIENCE INTO SILLY CARTOONS
articles.summary: In “Physics for Cats”, cartoonist Tom Gauld blends thoroughly researched science with absurd cat humor—drawing inspiration from The Far Side—to make complex ideas playful, accessible, and joyfully relatable for both scientists and everyday readers.
